findings.issue: Synthesis prompt loads all findings into memory with 12000-char slice, then discards unsliced rejected patterns — inconsistent truncation wastes tokens on rejected data that may exceed budget.
findings.suggestion: Slice rejected patterns to same 2000-char limit consistently, or implement priority-based truncation (findings first, patterns only if budget permits).

findings.issue: SCAN_SYSTEM prompt is set as ephemeral cache control but never re-used across different file scans — caching overhead exceeds benefit for single-use prompts.
findings.suggestion: Remove cache_control or batch scans under single message thread to amortize cache cost.

findings.issue: File content sliced to 45000 chars but no logging of truncation; downstream findings may reference lines beyond actual truncated content, creating false positives.
findings.suggestion: Log truncation event and add disclaimer to findings JSON or include actual_bytes_scanned field.
findings.est_impact: Silent data loss for large files

findings.issue: Delta scan reorders queue (modified files first) but SWEEPER_MAP last_scanned timestamps only updated after full batch completes — state inconsistency if batch interrupted.
findings.suggestion: Update last_scanned timestamps immediately after each file scan completes, not after full batch.

findings.issue: loadCosts() called twice per getCombinedDailyCost() invocation (once for ARCH_COSTS, once for SWEEPER_COSTS) — redundant file I/O.
findings.suggestion: Load both cost files once, merge them in getCombinedDailyCost().
findings.est_impact: 2 extra file reads per budget check
